summary: The Partial List Coloring Conjecture of Albertson, Grossman, and Haas remains open for general graphs. Post-2008 progress includes a proof of the conjecture for claw-free graphs, chordless graphs, series-parallel graphs, and graphs with chromatic number at least $(|V(G)|-1)/2$. The DP-coloring analogue of the conjecture has been shown to fail, indicating the conjecture does not follow from a more general principle.

since_posted.title: Partial List Colouring of Certain Graphs
since_posted.authors: Jeannette Janssen, Rogers Mathew, Deepak Rajendraprasad
since_posted.claim: Proves the conjecture for claw-free graphs, chordless graphs, series-parallel graphs, and graphs with chromatic number at least $(|V(G)|-1)/2$; also constructs an infinite family of 3-choosable graphs showing a related variant fails.

since_posted.title: Partial DP-Coloring
since_posted.authors: Hemanshu Kaul, Jeffrey A. Mudrock, Michael J. Pelsmajer
since_posted.claim: Initiates the study of partial DP-coloring; shows the DP-coloring analogue of the Partial List Coloring Conjecture does not hold, while several partial list coloring results do extend to the DP-coloring setting.

search_queries: partial list coloring conjecture Albertson Grossman Haas progress results
partial list coloring conjecture tn/chi_l vertices arXiv
list chromatic number partial coloring lower bound vertices colored conjecture proof
notes: The Chappell (1998) lower bound (arXiv:math/9805066) showing at least 6/7 of the conjectured number can be colored predates the OPG posting and is not included in since_posted. The Kaul et al. paper likely appeared in Discrete Mathematics in 2021 (ScienceDirect PII S0012365X21000194) but that page returned HTTP 403 and could not be verified directly; arXiv URL is cited instead. No post-2015 paper verifiably proving further new cases of the original conjecture was found. The conjecture is also known to hold for bipartite graphs (follows from t ≤ ch(G) − χ(G) + 1) and when t divides ch(G), though these results appear in the original or earlier literature.
